skill.name: risk-metrics-calculation
skill.description: Calculate portfolio risk metrics including VaR, CVaR, Sharpe, Sortino, volatility, beta, stress tests, and drawdown analysis.
skill.summary: Calculate and explain portfolio risk metrics for trading, allocation, and monitoring workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are documentation fences around Python examples, not executable commands. The hardcoded URLs are bibliography and documentation links, and no prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fences Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick locations are Markdown code fences around Python examples and a time horizon text block. They do not execute commands or request shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows these are Markdown fences and Python documentation snippets. No shell command, subprocess call, or user-controlled execution path is present at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: Reference Links to External Documentation
low_findings.description: The flagged URLs are resource links for books and pyfolio documentation. They are not active network calls, tracking endpoints, or data transfer logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links appear only under a Resources section and are standard reference URLs. No evidence found of automatic fetching or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a Financial Acronym False Positive
low_findings.description: The static blocker appears to match CVaR in the skill description, but the context is Conditional Value at Risk, not a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line describes VaR, CVaR, Sharpe, Sortino, and drawdown analysis. This is finance terminology, not cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Finding Is Finance Guidance False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged phrase is a best-practice warning about ignoring correlation during stressed markets. It does not describe scanning networks or hosts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is in the finance best-practices section and discusses asset correlation. No evidence found of network discovery behavior.

content.value_statement: Portfolio risk work often needs consistent calculations across volatility, tail loss, and drawdown measures. This skill provides practical Python patterns for measuring, summarizing, and monitoring portfolio risk.
actual_capabilities: Explains volatility, downside deviation, beta, VaR, CVaR, and drawdown metrics.
Provides Python class patterns for single-asset and portfolio risk calculations.
Shows rolling risk analysis for changing volatility and risk regimes.
Includes historical and hypothetical stress testing concepts.
Summarizes best practices for combining multiple risk measures.
limitations: The skill is guidance and example code, not a complete production library.
Examples require user-provided return series and clean market data.
It does not validate regulatory compliance requirements for specific jurisdictions.
Risk metrics can understate losses during new or extreme market regimes.

use_cases.title: Daily Portfolio Monitoring
use_cases.description: Build a repeatable report that tracks volatility, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, and Sharpe ratio for an active portfolio.

use_cases.title: Risk Limit Design
use_cases.description: Define measurable thresholds for tail loss, concentration, drawdown, and stress-period exposure before deploying a strategy.

use_cases.title: Quant Strategy Evaluation
use_cases.description: Compare strategies using rolling risk metrics, stress scenarios, and risk-adjusted return measures.

prompt_templates.title: Calculate Basic Risk Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to calculate volatility, Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, VaR, CVaR, and maximum drawdown for my return series.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Risk Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a portfolio risk summary that explains each metric, the assumptions used, and which metrics need the most attention.

prompt_templates.title: Design Rolling Risk Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design rolling 63-day and 252-day risk metrics for a portfolio dashboard, including threshold alerts.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test a Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define historical and hypothetical stress tests for my strategy and explain the expected risk report outputs.

output_examples.input: A portfolio manager asks for a daily risk report for equity returns.
output_examples.output: The skill can outline volatility, VaR, CVaR, drawdown, Sharpe, and Sortino sections with clear assumptions and interpretation notes.

output_examples.input: A quant developer wants to compare two strategies after backtesting.
output_examples.output: The skill can recommend side-by-side risk-adjusted metrics, rolling drawdown charts, stress-period returns, and tail-risk comparisons.

output_examples.input: A risk analyst needs alert thresholds for a trading dashboard.
output_examples.output: The skill can propose rolling volatility bands, VaR breach rules, drawdown limits, and escalation criteria.

best_practices: Use several risk metrics because each measure captures a different loss pattern.
Document confidence levels, lookback windows, data frequency, and risk-free rate assumptions.
Pair VaR with CVaR and stress tests to account for severe tail losses.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on VaR alone for tail-risk decisions.
Do not assume return distributions are normal without checking skew and kurtosis.
Do not use short lookback windows as the only input for long-term risk limits.

faq.question: What data does this skill need?
faq.answer: It works best with clean periodic return series for assets, benchmarks, or complete portfolios.

faq.question: Can it calculate Value at Risk?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes historical, parametric, and Cornish-Fisher VaR approaches.

faq.question: Does it support portfolio-level analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes portfolio weighting, contribution, correlation, and stress-period analysis concepts.

faq.question: Is this a trading signal generator?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on measuring risk and interpreting portfolio behavior.

faq.question: Can it replace professional risk systems?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples that require validation before production use.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
